title: Texas Car Accident Statute of Limitations — Plano (2026)
twitter: ⚠️ Plano drivers: Texas gives you just 2 YEARS to file a car accident lawsuit. Miss the deadline = lose your claim FOREVER. Know your rights before time runs out. linkedin:
If you were injured in a car accident in Plano, Texas, one deadline matters more than almost anything else: the 2-year statute of limitations under Texas Civil Practice & Remedies Code §16.003.

Miss it — even by a single day — and your right to compensation is gone. No negotiation. No second chances.

Yet every year, accident victims lose valid claims simply because they didn't know the clock was running from the moment of impact. There are narrow exceptions — government vehicles, minors, discovery rules — but relying on exceptions is a dangerous strategy without verified legal guidance.

At WreckMatch, we connect injured Texans with our network of 800+ vetted personal injury law firms who know Plano's courts, local insurers, and the procedural timelines that protect your claim.

Whether your accident was last week or last year, the time to act is now. A qualified attorney can confirm your exact deadline, preserve critical evidence, and build the strongest possible case before the window closes.

Don't gamble with your future. Get matched with a licensed Texas attorney today: https://www.wreckmatch.com/#form
facebook:
If you or someone you love was in a car accident in Plano, here's something every Texan needs to know: you generally have only 2 years from the date of the crash to file a personal injury lawsuit. That may sound like plenty of time, but between medical appointments, insurance calls, and just trying to recover — it passes faster than you think.

Miss that deadline and Texas law says your claim is gone. Forever. No exceptions for "I didn't know" or "I was still negotiating with the insurance company."

The good news? If you act now, there's still time to protect yourself. WreckMatch connects accident victims in Plano with experienced local attorneys who know exactly how to preserve your rights and maximize your recovery.

reddit_title: Got into a car accident in Plano, TX — how long do I actually have to file a lawsuit and what can reset the clock?
reddit_body:
Texas has a 2-year statute of limitations for personal injury claims under Tex. Civ. Prac. & Rem. Code §16.003, and it starts running on the date of your accident — not when you discover your injuries, not when negotiations with the insurer fall apart. Two years sounds like a lot until it isn't, especially when medical treatment and back-and-forth with adjusters eat up months quietly.

There are some exceptions worth knowing. If a government vehicle was involved (city bus, police car, municipal truck), you may have as little as 6 months to file a formal notice of claim before the lawsuit window even opens. If the injured person is a minor, the clock typically doesn't start until they turn 18. The "discovery rule" can sometimes delay the start date when injuries weren't immediately apparent, but courts apply it narrowly — don't count on it as a fallback plan.

Bottom line: verify your specific deadline with a licensed Texas attorney as soon as possible, even if you're still deciding whether to pursue a claim. Many offer free consultations. If you want help finding someone local to Plano quickly, wreckmatch.com matches accident victims with vetted personal injury attorneys in their area — no commitment required.
